[the problem is to find the maximum value of the function u = log_{1/2}(8xy + 4y² + 1) under the constraints x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0, and x + 2y = 1/2]  
[the constraints x ≥ 0, y ≥ 0, and x + 2y = 1/2 define a region in the xy-plane, and the function u is defined for all x and y in this region]  
[the function u is a logarithmic function with base 1/2, which is a decreasing function, so maximizing u is equivalent to minimizing the argument of the logarithm]  
[the argument of the logarithm is 8xy + 4y² + 1, which is a quadratic expression in terms of x and y]  
[the expression 8xy + 4y² + 1 can be simplified using the constraint x + 2y = 1/2 to find its minimum value]  
[using the constraint x = 1/2 - 2y, substitute into the expression to find the minimum value of 8xy + 4y² + 1]  
[after substitution, the expression becomes 8(1/2 - 2y)y + 4y² + 1, which simplifies to 4y - 16y² + 4y² + 1]  
[the expression simplifies further to 4y - 12y² + 1, which is a quadratic function in terms of y]  
[the minimum value of the quadratic function occurs at the vertex, which is at y = -b/(2a) = -4/(2*(-12)) = 1/6]  
[substituting y = 1/6 back into the expression gives the minimum value of 4*(1/6) - 12*(1/6)² + 1 = 2/3 - 12*(1/36) + 1 = 2/3 - 1/3 + 1 = 2/3 + 2/3 = 4/3]  
[the minimum value of the argument of the logarithm is 4/3, so the maximum value of u is log_{1/2}(4/3)]
